architecture.name: MedSecure Healthcare SaaS
architecture.description: Enterprise healthcare SaaS platform serving hospitals, clinics, and payers. Multi-tenant AKS deployment with FHIR R4 interoperability, PHI-hardened data tier, and EHR integrations with Epic and Cerner. All PHI encrypted at rest (AES-256) and in transit (TLS 1.3). HIPAA BAA in place with Azure.

components.name: Angular 17 SPA (Patient Portal)
components.technology: Angular 17.1, TypeScript 5.3, RxJS 7.8, Angular Material 17.1
components.notes: Patient-facing portal for scheduling, lab results, billing, and messaging. CSP enforced. No PHI stored in localStorage.

components.name: Azure Front Door CDN
components.technology: Azure Front Door Standard Tier (AFD-Standard-AzureFrontDoor)
components.notes: Global CDN and load balancer. Anycast routing. TLS 1.3 termination. DDoS protection included. No PHI cached at edge.

components.name: Azure WAF
components.technology: Azure Web Application Firewall (DRS 2.1 managed ruleset + custom OWASP rules)
components.notes: OWASP Top 10 blocking mode. Custom rules for HIPAA-specific attack patterns (SQL injection targeting PHI fields, path traversal).

components.name: Azure Application Gateway v2
components.technology: Azure Application Gateway v2 (WAF_v2 SKU), Zone-redundant
components.notes: Layer 7 load balancer with SSL offload, cookie-based session affinity, and URL-based routing to AKS ingress. Health probes per service.

components.name: AKS Cluster (Application Workloads)
components.technology: Azure Kubernetes Service 1.28.5, containerd 1.7, Azure CNI Overlay, Calico network policies
components.notes: System node pool: Standard_D4s_v5 (4 vCPU, 16 GB). User node pool: Standard_D8s_v5 (8 vCPU, 32 GB), auto-scale 3-15. Private cluster endpoint. Azure Defender for Containers enabled.

components.name: Azure API Management
components.technology: Azure API Management (Premium tier, v2 gateway), Developer Portal enabled
components.notes: Centralized API gateway. JWT validation, rate limiting (1000 req/min/tenant), OAuth 2.0 enforcement, API versioning, request/response transformation. mTLS to backend services.

components.name: FHIR API Service (HL7 FHIR R4)
components.technology: Azure Health Data Services — FHIR Service R4 (managed), SMART on FHIR 2.0
components.notes: HL7 FHIR R4 compliant. Supports Patient, Observation, Encounter, DiagnosticReport, MedicationRequest, Condition, Procedure, AllergyIntolerance resources. SMART on FHIR scopes enforced per resource type.

components.name: Patient Data Service
components.technology: .NET 8.0 (ASP.NET Core 8.0.1), C# 12, Polly 8.3, MediatR 12.2
components.notes: Core patient demographics, registration, insurance eligibility. PHI fields encrypted at column level with Azure Key Vault-derived keys. Audit log on every read/write.

components.name: Clinical Workflow Service
components.technology: .NET 8.0 (ASP.NET Core 8.0.1), C# 12, Dapr 1.12
components.notes: Manages clinical orders, care plans, encounter workflows, discharge summaries. Integrates with FHIR Service for resource persistence. Role-based access: physician, nurse, care coordinator.

components.name: Lab Results Service
components.technology: .NET 8.0 (ASP.NET Core 8.0.1), C# 12, HL7 v2.5.1 parser (NHapi 3.0)
components.notes: Ingests HL7 v2 lab messages from LIS systems, transforms to FHIR DiagnosticReport, stores in FHIR Service. Notifies patients on result availability. Critical result alerting.

components.name: Scheduling Service
components.technology: .NET 8.0 (ASP.NET Core 8.0.1), C# 12, Quartz.NET 3.8
components.notes: Patient appointment scheduling, provider calendar management, slot availability. Sends appointment reminders via Notification Service. Integrates with Epic slots API.

components.name: Billing Service
components.technology: .NET 8.0 (ASP.NET Core 8.0.1), C# 12, X12 EDI 837P/835 parser
components.notes: Medical billing, insurance claims (X12 EDI 837P/I), remittance (835), patient statements, ERA processing. PCI-scoped for payment card data. HIPAA transaction standards enforced.

components.name: Notification Service
components.technology: Azure Functions v4 (.NET 8.0 isolated worker), Twilio SDK 6.10, SendGrid 9.29
components.notes: Sends appointment reminders (SMS/email), lab result alerts, billing statements, care plan updates. PHI in notifications minimized. BAA in place with Twilio and SendGrid.

components.name: Imaging Service (DICOM)
components.technology: .NET 8.0, fo-dicom 5.1, Azure Health Data Services — DICOM Service (DICOMweb API)
components.notes: DICOM medical image storage and retrieval via DICOMweb (WADO-RS, STOW-RS, QIDO-RS). Thumbnail generation for patient portal. Access control per patient MRN. Images stored in Azure Blob.

components.name: Azure Cosmos DB (Patient Records)
components.technology: Azure Cosmos DB for NoSQL, API: NoSQL, Consistency: Session, RU/s: 50,000 autoscale
components.notes: Primary PHI store for patient demographics, encounter summaries, preferences. Multi-region writes (eastus2 primary, westus2 secondary). CMK encryption via Azure Key Vault. Point-in-time restore 30 days.

components.name: Azure SQL Database (Operational Data)
components.technology: Azure SQL Database Hyperscale (Gen5, 8 vCores), SQL Server 2022 compatibility
components.notes: Relational store for scheduling, billing, clinical orders, audit logs. TDE with CMK. Always Encrypted for PHI columns (SSN, DOB, MRN). Geo-redundant backup. Dynamic data masking for non-privileged roles.

components.name: Azure Blob Storage (PHI Documents & Images)
components.technology: Azure Blob Storage (ZRS, Hot + Cool tiers), Azure Data Lake Storage Gen2
components.notes: Stores consent forms, scanned documents, medical images (DICOM), exported reports. CMK encryption. Private endpoint only. Lifecycle management: 90 days Hot, 1 year Cool, 7 year Archive per HIPAA retention. Immutable storage for audit logs.

components.name: Azure Event Hub (Clinical Events)
components.technology: Azure Event Hubs Premium Tier, Apache Kafka 3.6 compatible, 10 partitions
components.notes: Real-time clinical event stream: lab result received, appointment booked, order placed, discharge event. Consumed by FHIR integrations and downstream analytics. PHI in event payloads encrypted with CMK. Capture to Blob Storage for 7-year retention.

components.name: Azure Logic Apps (FHIR Integration)
components.technology: Azure Logic Apps Standard (WS1 plan), HL7 v2 connector, FHIR connector
components.notes: Orchestrates HL7 v2 to FHIR R4 transformation workflows. Handles ADT (patient admission/discharge/transfer), ORM (orders), ORU (results), SIU (scheduling) message types. Run history stored with PHI controls.

components.name: Azure Key Vault (PHI Encryption Keys)
components.technology: Azure Key Vault Premium (HSM-backed, FIPS 140-2 Level 3), Managed HSM optional
components.notes: Stores CMKs for Cosmos DB, SQL, Blob Storage, Event Hubs. Also stores TLS certificates, service-to-service secrets, EHR integration credentials. 90-day key rotation enforced. Soft delete + purge protection enabled. Accessed via Managed Identity only.

components.name: Azure Active Directory B2C (Patient Identity)
components.technology: Azure AD B2C, OpenID Connect 1.0, OAuth 2.0, PKCE, MFA via TOTP/SMS
components.notes: Patient identity and access management. Custom user flows for registration, login, MFA, password reset. SMART on FHIR scope issuance. Provider identity via Azure AD federated to hospital SSO (SAML 2.0 / OIDC).

components.name: Azure Monitor + Application Insights
components.technology: Azure Monitor, Application Insights SDK (.NET 8), Log Analytics Workspace, Azure Sentinel
components.notes: Platform-wide telemetry, APM, distributed tracing, HIPAA audit log aggregation. PHI scrubbed from telemetry via custom TelemetryInitializer before ingestion. Sentinel SIEM for threat detection. 365-day log retention per HIPAA.

components.name: Azure Service Bus (Async Messaging)
components.technology: Azure Service Bus Premium Tier (1 Messaging Unit), geo-disaster recovery enabled
components.notes: Dead-letter queue for failed clinical events. Topics: clinical-orders, billing-events, notification-dispatch, ehr-sync. CMK encryption. Sessions for ordered PHI processing. Message TTL 7 days.

components.name: Azure Cache for Redis
components.technology: Azure Cache for Redis Enterprise (E10 SKU, 10 GB), Redis 7.2, TLS 1.3
components.notes: Session cache, API rate limit counters, provider schedule lookups, non-PHI reference data. PHI is NOT stored in Redis. ACL per service account. In-transit TLS enforced, CMK-encrypted persistent RDB snapshots.

components.name: Azure Container Registry
components.technology: Azure Container Registry Premium (Geo-replication: eastus2, westus2), Defender for ACR
components.notes: Stores all Docker images. Defender for Containers scans on push. Private endpoint only. Token-based authentication for AKS pull. Image signing with Notation (CNCF).

components.name: Azure Managed Identity (Workload Identity)
components.technology: Azure Workload Identity Federation, Managed Identity per AKS workload, AAD Pod Identity v2
components.notes: Each AKS service workload gets a dedicated Managed Identity scoped to minimum required Azure resources. No credentials in code or environment variables. Federated credentials for GitHub Actions CI/CD.

components.name: Azure Defender for Cloud
components.technology: Microsoft Defender for Cloud (CSPM + CWP), Defender for Containers, Defender for SQL
components.notes: Cloud Security Posture Management for HIPAA regulatory compliance assessment. Defender for Containers monitors AKS runtime. Defender for SQL on Azure SQL. Secure Score tracking. Alerts feed to Azure Sentinel.

components.name: Epic EHR Integration Endpoint
components.technology: Epic FHIR R4 API (Epic on FHIR), SMART on FHIR, OAuth 2.0 backend workflow
components.notes: Epic Systems EHR. Patient data syndication, appointment slots, clinical orders, CCDs. BAA with Epic covered under hospital HIPAA agreement. mTLS with Epic-issued client certificates. Rate limit: 10,000 req/day per customer.

components.name: Cerner EHR Integration Endpoint
components.technology: Oracle Health (Cerner) Millennium FHIR R4 API, SMART on FHIR 2.0, CDS Hooks 2.0
components.notes: Oracle Health (Cerner Millennium) EHR. Patient chart sync, medication lists, problem list, allergy data. CDS Hooks for clinical decision support. BAA under hospital agreement. TLS 1.2+ enforced.

components.name: Azure Policy + Blueprint (Compliance Controls)
components.technology: Azure Policy (HIPAA/HITRUST built-in initiative), Azure Blueprints v2, Defender for DevOps
components.notes: Enforces HIPAA HITRUST policy initiative across all subscriptions. Deny policies: public endpoints, non-CMK storage, non-TLS services. Audit policies feed Compliance Manager. Blueprints lock critical resource configurations.

components.name: Azure Private DNS Zones
components.technology: Azure Private DNS Zones (privatelink.*.azure.com), Azure DNS Resolver
components.notes: Private DNS resolution for all private endpoints (Cosmos DB, SQL, Blob, Key Vault, Event Hubs, Service Bus). Prevents public DNS resolution of PHI data stores. Conditional forwarder for on-premises hybrid scenarios.

components.name: Azure Virtual Network + Private Endpoints
components.technology: Azure VNet (10.0.0.0/16), NSG, Private Endpoints for all PaaS, VNet peering
components.notes: Hub-spoke topology. Spokes: App (10.0.1.0/24), Data (10.0.2.0/24), Integration (10.0.3.0/24). Private endpoints for: Cosmos DB, SQL, Blob, Event Hubs, Service Bus, Key Vault, ACR. No PaaS services on public internet.

connections.description: Patient Portal SPA served and cached via Azure Front Door CDN
Front Door routes all inbound requests through Azure WAF for OWASP inspection
WAF-inspected traffic forwarded to Application Gateway v2
Application Gateway routes API traffic to Azure API Management
API Management routes FHIR resource requests to FHIR API Service
API Management routes /patients/* to Patient Data Service
API Management routes /clinical/* to Clinical Workflow Service
API Management routes /labs/* to Lab Results Service
API Management routes /scheduling/* to Scheduling Service
API Management routes /billing/* to Billing Service
API Management routes /imaging/* to Imaging Service (DICOMweb)
AKS pulls container images from Azure Container Registry via private endpoint
FHIR Service reads/writes FHIR resources stored in Cosmos DB
FHIR Service retrieves PHI encryption keys from Azure Key Vault via Managed Identity
Patient Data Service reads/writes patient demographics to Cosmos DB
Patient Data Service uses Key Vault for column-level PHI encryption keys
Clinical Workflow Service reads/writes clinical orders to Azure SQL Database
Clinical Workflow Service publishes encounter events to Event Hubs
Lab Results Service posts DiagnosticReport FHIR resources to FHIR Service
Lab Results Service enqueues result-ready notifications to Service Bus
Scheduling Service reads/writes appointment records to Azure SQL Database
Scheduling Service syncs appointment slots with Epic EHR API
Billing Service reads/writes claims, remittances, and statements to Azure SQL
Billing Service publishes billing events to Service Bus for downstream processing
Notification Service reads template documents from Azure Blob Storage
Notification Service consumes notification-dispatch topic from Service Bus
Imaging Service stores and retrieves DICOM files from Azure Blob Storage
Imaging Service retrieves Blob Storage CMK from Key Vault
Logic Apps posts HL7-to-FHIR transformed resources to FHIR Service
Logic Apps polls Cerner EHR for patient data and clinical updates
Logic Apps syncs CCD documents and problem lists with Epic EHR
AD B2C issues JWT tokens validated by API Management on all requests
Azure Monitor collects AKS metrics, logs, and container insights
Azure Monitor collects SQL Database audit logs and performance metrics
Azure Monitor collects Cosmos DB metrics and diagnostic logs
Patient Data Service reads non-PHI session and reference data from Redis Cache
Scheduling Service caches provider availability windows in Redis
Workload Identity Managed Identity grants AKS services access to Key Vault secrets
Defender for Cloud security alerts fed to Azure Monitor / Sentinel SIEM
Azure Policy enforces VNet, private endpoint, and CMK requirements at subscription scope
Private DNS Zones resolve private endpoint FQDNs within VNet for all PHI data stores
Event Hubs streams clinical events to Logic Apps for FHIR transformation and EHR sync

trust_boundaries.name: Internet (Untrusted Public Zone)
controls: TLS 1.3 enforced (TLS 1.0/1.1 rejected)
Azure DDoS Protection Standard
Azure WAF DRS 2.1 in Prevention mode
Bot Manager ruleset
Geo-IP filtering (configurable per tenant)
HSTS with preload
Content Security Policy headers
No PHI cached at CDN edge

trust_boundaries.name: DMZ (Edge to Application Gateway)
controls: Azure Application Gateway v2 (WAF_v2 SKU)
SSL/TLS re-encryption to backend (end-to-end TLS)
NSG: allow 443 inbound only from Front Door service tag
Private IP only for Application Gateway backend pool
OAuth 2.0 token validation at API Management boundary
JWT signature verification (RS256, Azure AD B2C JWKS)

trust_boundaries.name: Application Tier (AKS Private Cluster)
controls: AKS private cluster (control plane not internet-exposed)
Calico NetworkPolicy: default-deny, allow-listed service-to-service only
Azure CNI Overlay with Pod-level NSGs
Workload Identity (Managed Identity per service, no secrets in pods)
Pod Security Standards (restricted profile): no privilege escalation, read-only root FS
RBAC per namespace with Kubernetes RBAC + Azure AD integration
mTLS between services via Istio service mesh (STRICT mode)
OPA/Gatekeeper admission controller: enforce labels, resource limits, approved images
Defender for Containers: runtime threat detection, vulnerability scanning

trust_boundaries.name: PHI Data Tier (Private Endpoints Only)
controls: All PHI data stores accessible via private endpoint ONLY (no public access)
Private DNS Zones resolve *.privatelink.*.azure.com within VNet
Azure SQL: firewall deny all public; Allow Azure services: OFF; private endpoint only
Cosmos DB: public network access disabled, CMK encryption required
Blob Storage: public access blocked, private endpoint, SAS tokens forbidden (Managed Identity only)
Event Hubs: IP filter deny all, private endpoint, CMK encryption
Service Bus: public access disabled, private endpoint, CMK
Encryption at rest: AES-256 with Customer-Managed Keys (Azure Key Vault HSM)
Encryption in transit: TLS 1.3 minimum
Always Encrypted (Deterministic/Randomized) for SSN, DOB, MRN columns in SQL
Immutable storage (WORM) for audit logs: 7-year retention per HIPAA
Azure Defender for SQL: runtime threat detection on Azure SQL
Cosmos DB: IP rules restrict to AKS subnet CIDR only

trust_boundaries.name: External EHR Systems (Third-Party PHI Exchange)
controls: mTLS with EHR-issued client certificates for Epic
OAuth 2.0 backend service authorization for Cerner (client_credentials flow)
SMART on FHIR scopes: minimum necessary PHI access principle enforced
Azure Firewall FQDN allowlisting: only Epic/Cerner API endpoints permitted outbound
No PHI persisted in Logic Apps run history (secure outputs enabled)
BAA (Business Associate Agreement) in place with Epic and Cerner via hospital customer
Rate limiting enforced to prevent data exfiltration at scale
Audit trail for all EHR data exchanges stored in Log Analytics (365-day retention)
Connection via Azure Virtual Network NAT Gateway (static egress IP allowlisted at Epic/Cerner)

data_flows.name: Patient Authentication Flow
data_flows.description: Patient logs in via Angular SPA. Azure AD B2C issues PKCE-based OAuth2 authorization code. OIDC ID token + access token returned. Access token contains SMART on FHIR scopes. API Management validates JWT on every subsequent API call.
controls: PKCE flow prevents authorization code interception
Short-lived access tokens (1 hour)
Refresh token rotation
MFA enforced for PHI access

data_flows.name: Patient Record Retrieval (PHI Read)
data_flows.description: Authenticated patient requests their health record. API Management validates JWT and SMART patient/*.read scope. Patient Data Service fetches demographics from Cosmos DB. Column-level decryption for PHI fields using CMK from Key Vault.
controls: JWT validation with scope enforcement
PHI column decryption via Key Vault CMK
HIPAA audit log: who accessed what PHI and when
TLS 1.3 end-to-end

data_flows.name: Lab Result Publication (PHI Write + Notification)
data_flows.description: HL7 ORU message arrives from LIS via Logic Apps. Lab Results Service transforms to FHIR DiagnosticReport and posts to FHIR Service. Result stored in Cosmos DB. Critical result check triggers immediate notification via Service Bus to Azure Functions. Patient notified via email/SMS (PHI minimized in notification body).
controls: HL7 message authentication
FHIR resource validation
PHI minimization in notification payloads
Service Bus CMK
Notification BAA with Twilio/SendGrid

data_flows.name: DICOM Image Upload and Storage (PHI Write)
data_flows.description: Imaging modality pushes DICOM study via DICOMweb STOW-RS API. Imaging Service stores DICOM objects in Azure Blob Storage with CMK encryption. Patient MRN embedded in DICOM tags is treated as PHI. Access restricted to treating clinicians and the patient themselves.
controls: DICOMweb STOW-RS authentication
Azure Blob private endpoint
CMK encryption at rest
Access control per patient MRN
Audit log on DICOM object access

data_flows.name: EHR Synchronization (PHI Cross-System Exchange)
data_flows.description: Scheduled and event-driven sync pulls patient CCD, medication lists, problem lists, and allergies from Epic and Cerner EHRs via Logic Apps. Logic Apps transforms to FHIR R4 resources and upserts into FHIR Service. Cosmos DB stores canonical patient record.
controls: mTLS to Epic
OAuth2 backend flow to Cerner
SMART scopes: minimum necessary
No PHI in Logic Apps run history (secure outputs)
Azure Firewall FQDN filtering outbound

data_flows.name: Clinical Event Stream (Real-Time PHI Events)
data_flows.description: Clinical Workflow Service emits encounter events (admit, discharge, order, update) to Event Hubs. Logic Apps consumes stream for real-time EHR synchronization. Events contain patient_id (de-identified token); full PHI fetched from FHIR Service using token.
controls: Event Hub CMK
Patient ID tokenization (not MRN in event payload)
AMQP TLS 1.3
Event capture to Blob Storage with immutable retention

data_flows.name: Billing Claims Submission (PHI + Financial)
data_flows.description: Billing Service generates X12 837P/I claims from clinical encounters in Azure SQL. Claims contain PHI (patient name, diagnosis codes, procedure codes, NPI). Submitted to payer clearinghouse via Service Bus integration. ERA 835 remittances processed back into SQL.
controls: Always Encrypted SQL columns for PHI
Service Bus CMK
X12 EDI validation before submission
HIPAA transaction set compliance (837/835/271/272)
Audit log per claim

hipaa_baa_scope.description: All components that store, process, or transmit PHI and are covered under the Azure HIPAA BAA

hitrust_csf_scope.description: Components assessed against HITRUST CSF v11.2 control categories
control_categories: 10.f — Policy on Use of Cryptographic Controls

fhir_r4_interoperability_scope.description: Components participating in HL7 FHIR R4 data exchange and ONC interoperability requirements

soc2_type_ii_scope.description: Components in scope for SOC 2 Type II audit across Trust Service Criteria: Security, Availability, Confidentiality, Processing Integrity, Privacy

phi_data_stores.description: Authoritative list of all Azure resources storing PHI at rest

stores.phi_type: Patient demographics, encounter summaries, FHIR resources
Clinical orders, scheduling, billing claims, audit logs
DICOM images, consent forms, clinical documents, exported reports
stores.retention: Hot 90 days, Cool 1 year, Archive 7 years (WORM)
7 days hot + 7 years via Capture to Blob

stores.phi_type: In-flight billing events, notification payloads (PHI minimized)
